title: audit — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for audit-adjacent operations. Thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic. Callers who need the primary query surface use the AuditQuery bound service; helpers here are for ad-hoc scripts + platform-support tooling.

helpers.purpose: Convenience — returns a fresh AuditQuery instance bound to the current auth context. Consumer chains fluent filters: audit_query()->byUser($userId)->since('-7 days')->limit(50)->get(). Wraps app()->make(AuditQuery::class).

Returns the effective retention_years for a tenant. Reads the active AuditRetentionPolicy; falls back to config.audit.retention.default_years (7) when no policy is active. Reads through the audit:retention:{tenant_id} cache.

Applies the configured PII redaction pattern set (compiled at boot) to a diff blob. Replaces matching values with SHA-256 hashes. Consumers rarely call this directly — it runs inside AuditObserver::creating when config.audit.compliance.redact_pii_in_old_new_values=true.

Generates a fresh signed URL for an AuditExport's S3 object. TTL defaults to config.audit.export.signed_url_ttl_hours * 3600. Rate-limited to 3 regenerations per export (chain-of-custody — a signed URL is a bearer token, so uncontrolled regeneration is a threat model).

Downloads the manifest.json from S3, verifies the HMAC-SHA256 signature against the platform's regulator-signing key (from Doppler), computes the SHA-256 of the payload.jsonl.gz object, compares to the manifest's declared sha256. Returns { valid: bool, mismatches: array<string> }. Used by regulator investigators to prove chain-of-custody.

Returns the closed set of event names valid for a given auditable model class (vendor defaults + registered custom transitions for that model). Read from the audit:audited_events_catalog cache. Used by the SDUI filter surface to populate the event-name dropdown per selected auditable_type.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub + every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services. Keeps the boundary clean + preserves the read-only-over-HTTP contract.
